project_info.title: Mathematical Foundations for AI Engineering: Row-Echelon Form & Gauss-Jordan Elimination

outline.title: Mathematical Foundations for AI Engineering: Row-Echelon Form & Gauss-Jordan Elimination
outline.summary: Title slide with accreditation to Adrian S. Obungu and social links.

outline.title: Introduction & Module Overview
outline.summary: Briefly introduces the core concepts of Row-Echelon Form (REF) and Reduced Row-Echelon Form (RREF) as foundational elements of AI engineering.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Core Logic: REF vs. RREF
outline.summary: Explains the mathematical conditions for a matrix to be in REF and RREF, emphasizing the uniqueness of the RREF.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: The Gauss-Jordan Elimination Process
outline.summary: Outlines the algorithmic steps of using elementary row operations to reach RREF.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Active Lab: Implementing Gauss-Jordan in Python
outline.summary: Introduces the Active Lab and its objective: to bridge the gap between mathematical theory and computational implementation.
outline.image_plan: Use a suitable image if needed

outline.title: Complexity Level 1: Conceptual Foundations
outline.summary: Discusses the basic understanding of the algorithm's outcome and its real-world relevance in high-level AI libraries.

outline.title: Complexity Level 2: Step-by-Step Implementation
outline.summary: Explores the manual execution of elementary row operations in code for debugging and deep algorithmic understanding.

outline.title: Complexity Level 3: Advanced Numerical Analysis
outline.summary: Addresses numerical stability, pivot selection strategies, and their importance in high-precision scientific computing.

outline.title: Interpreting RREF: Solutions & Variables
outline.summary: Explains how to identify unique solutions, inconsistent systems, and infinite solutions (with basic and free variables) from the RREF.

outline.title: Join the Journey: The 'Learn Along' Protocol
outline.summary: Invites the audience to engage with the GitHub repository, fork the project, and start their own AI engineering learning path.
